name: your-inner-fish
description: Neil Shubin's Your Inner Fish — paleontology and evolutionary biology toolkit revealing how the 3.5-billion-year history of life is written in our bodies, anchored in the discovery of Tiktaalik, the fossil fish that walked, and tracing our hands, ears, and genes back to ancient fish
useCase: Understand human evolution through fossils, learn how our bodies reveal our fish ancestry, and explore the genetic and developmental evidence for common descent
knownRisks: The book was published in 2008 — some genetic and fossil discoveries have been made since
Shubin's teaching-through-fish-anatomy approach may not suit all learning styles
Evolution is a politicized topic in some contexts — the book presents the scientific consensus
